When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.

Level 2: you have used 94 actions; a strong score needs about 50 or fewer. You are 1.9x over the target.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
SPACE didn't complete the level. The dark gray is at cols 21-32 and the player is at cols 33-47. They're adjacent but not fully overlapping.
Let me think about this differently. The dark gray and player are L-shapes. Maybe the goal is to get them to form a specific pattern when they overlap.
Looking at the shapes:
- Dark gray L-shape: horizontal bar on top (rows 18-19), vertical bar going down on the right (cols 29-32)
- Player L-shape: horizontal bar on top (rows 18-19), vertical bar going down on the right (cols 41-47)
Wait, they're both L-shapes with the same orientation. If I move the dark gray to exactly match the player's position, they would overlap perfectly.
The dark gray is at cols 21-32 and the player is at cols 33-47. The gap is 1 column (32 to 33). If I do 1 more LEFT, the dark gray will move to cols 27-38, overlapping with the player at cols 33-38.
But the player might move again when the dark gray crosses the divider. The divider is at cols 31-33. The dark gray is at cols 21-32, already crossing the divider.
Actually, wait. Let me reconsider the player movement rule. The player moved when the dark gray crossed the divider. But now the dark gray is already on the right side of the divider. Maybe the player won't move anymore.
Let me try 1 more LEFT to get the dark gray closer to the player.
Actually, I've been spending too many actions on this. Let me think about what the goal might be.
In level 1, the goal was to overlap the dark gray with the yellow. The dark gray and yellow were on the same side.
In level 2, the dark gray and yellow are on the same side (left), but the player is on the right side. Maybe the goal is to move the dark gray to the player's side and then overlap with the player.
But I already tried overlapping with the player and it didn't complete the level.
Wait, maybe the goal is to move the dark gray to the EXACT same position as the player. The dark gray is at cols 21-32 and the player is at cols 33-47. They're not at the same position.
Let me try moving the dark gray to exactly match the player's position. The dark gray spans cols 21-32 (width 12) and the player spans cols 33-47 (width 15). Wait, the widths are different. Let me check the pixel counts.
- Dark gray: 63 pixels
- Player: 64 pixels
They're almost the same size. The difference might be due to the border.
Actually, looking at the boundaries:
- Dark gray: [[18, 27], [18, 32], [20, 32], [20, 30], [21, 29], [29, 29], [29, 21], [27, 21], [27, 26], [26, 27]]
  - This is an L-shape: horizontal bar at rows 18-19, cols 27-32; vertical bar at rows 18-29, cols 29-32 (but with a cutout)
  - Actually, let me parse this more carefully.
- Player: [[18, 33], [18, 41], [26, 41], [27, 42], [27, 47], [29, 47], [29, 39], [21, 39], [20, 38], [20, 33]]
  - This is also an L-shape.
Hmm, the shapes look similar but not identical. Let me try a different approach.
Maybe the goal is to move the dark gray to the yellow's position. The dark gray is at rows 18-29, cols 21-32. The yellow is at rows 42-53, cols 3-17. They're at different positions.
Wait, I just realized something. The dark gray and yellow are both on the LEFT side of the divider. The player is on the RIGHT side. In level 1, the dark gray and yellow were on the RIGHT side, and the player was on the LEFT side.
Maybe the goal in level 2 is to move the dark gray to the RIGHT side (where the player is) and then it will "swap" with the yellow or something.
Actually, let me try a completely different approach. Let me use ACTION7 to see what it does in the current state.

<function=python>
<parameter=code>

# Dark gray at cols 21-32, player at cols 33-47
action(['ACTION7'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
